---
title: "Executive Governance Series"
subtitle: "Long-form governance narrative in nine chapters"
doc-type: section-landing
audience: "Authorizing officials · CIO · CISO · program leadership"
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
aspirational: true
---

# Executive Governance Series {.unnumbered}

Book-length governance narrative written for authorizing officials and
program leadership. Each chapter is a standalone document with its own
directory; they can be read in sequence as a program primer or drawn on
individually as reference material.

## Chapters

| # | Slug | Topic |
|---|---|---|
| 00 | `00-introduction/` | Scope, audience, reading path |
| 01 | `01-modernization-arc/` | The federal modernization arc UIAO addresses |
| 02 | `02-governance-os-overview/` | What a governance OS is, and what it is not |
| 03 | `03-boundary-impact-model/` | Authorization boundary implications |
| 04 | `04-evidence-chain/` | Provenance model and OSCAL outputs |
| 05 | `05-governance-through-specification-and-validation/` | Spec-and-validate governance pattern |
| 06 | `06-program-model/` | How to run a UIAO-anchored program |
| 07 | `07-leadership-alignment/` | Stakeholder alignment and escalation paths |
| 08 | `08-executive-summary/` | Condensed brief for CIO / CISO |
